body.title: [sprint] CLAUDE.md:180 documents _launch_builder auto-launch as a live 'later path' — the definition is gone
body.intention: I intend that CLAUDE.md never describes a code path that does not exist, so that the document which arbitrates every conflict cannot itself be the source of one.
body.description:
CLAUDE.md line 180: 'Builder auto-launch (`launch_cmd` via `_launch_builder`, rate-limited) is a separate, *later* path for workers not already running; it does not apply to CC.0.' Line 169 also refers to 'the subprocess.Popen in _launch_builder'.

Verified 2026-07-09: `grep -rn 'def _launch_builder' unseen_university/` returns NOTHING. The definition is gone. granny/dispatch.py is retired and its own docstring says the opposite: 'Spawning CC directly violates two standing rules... must NEVER subprocess.Popen one.' T-granny-dispatch-rewrite's close note records the removal: 'Granny no longer launches any worker — removed _launch_builder, its run_once launch block, _last_launch_attempt...'

This is not cosmetic. CLAUDE.md is the document that arbitrates conflicts between the contract and the tree. A stale spawn path in it means the arbiter itself carries a false fact — and a future CC reading 'auto-launch is a separate, later path' would reasonably build it. Same failure class as the IMAP claim that CLAUDE.md now flags in bold ('repeatedly misleads CC').

**Affected files:** CLAUDE.md (lines ~169 and ~180). Possibly granny/dispatch.py (delete the retired module, or keep it as a tombstone — decide, don't drift).
**Design rules:** cooperative peers, each component responsible for itself (a spawner owns the spawnee's lifecycle = supervisor); feedback_granny_no_cc_spawn; validity_conditions (this entry should have carried one naming _launch_builder, and did not).
**Scope boundary:** IN — correct the two CLAUDE.md lines to state that no spawn path exists and that dispatch is bus-handshake only; attach a validity_condition naming the symbol so the day-close sweep catches it if it ever returns. OUT — deleting granny/dispatch.py (separate call); the claim-primitive gap (T-cc-queue-next-is-a-claim-primitive).
**Test plan:** a discriminating test, not a presence check: assert that `def _launch_builder` appears nowhere in unseen_university/ AND that CLAUDE.md contains no sentence describing auto-launch as an available path. Red form: at HEAD the second assertion fails.
**Completion criteria:** CLAUDE.md describes no spawn path; a validity_condition names _launch_builder so its reappearance is flagged by the day-close sweep.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.completion_criteria: CLAUDE.md has no live-auto-launch sentence; a validity_condition names the _launch_builder symbol; test asserts both the symbol's absence and the doc's silence.
